gbtmkit 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
    • Getting started with gbtmkit
  • Changelog

Fit the final chosen trajectory model

Source: R/fit.R
fit_gbtm.Rd

A thin wrapper over gbtm_fit() intended for the final model, once the number of groups and the polynomial shape have been chosen. It differs only in defaulting hessian = TRUE, so the result includes standard errors.

Usage

fit_gbtm(
  spec,
  n_groups,
  degrees,
  method = NULL,
  engine = gbtm_engines(),
  hessian = TRUE,
  itermax = 100L,
  seed = NULL,
  ...
)

Arguments

spec

A gbtm_spec.

n_groups

Number of latent groups.

degrees

Integer vector of polynomial degrees, length n_groups.

method

Estimation method; must be one of gbtm_engine_methods() for the chosen engine (ignored by engines with a single optimizer).

engine

Engine name; see gbtm_engines().

hessian

Logical; compute the Hessian (standard errors). Default TRUE.

itermax

Maximum optimizer iterations.

seed

Optional integer seed for reproducibility.

...

Passed on to the underlying engine call.

Value

A gbtm_fit object.

See also

gbtm_fit(), gbtm_diagnostics(), gbtm_assign(), plot_trajectories()

Examples

# \donttest{
data("sim_binary", package = "gbtmkit")
spec <- gbtm_spec(sim_binary, paste0("y", 1:10),
                  paste0("t", 1:10), id = "id", family = "binomial")
if (requireNamespace("trajeR", quietly = TRUE))
  fit_gbtm(spec, n_groups = 4, degrees = rep(3, 4), method = "L")
#> Starting Values
#> 0.250.250.250.25-5000-0.4775644582738760000.849072740011534000-5000
#> 
#> 
#> Likelihood
#> iter   1 value 
#> 10985.536114
#> iter   2 value 
#> 10980.527072
#> iter   3 value 
#> 10975.535925

#> 10974.685937
